channel.title: CyberWatch Threat Intelligence
channel.description: Aggregated CVEs, advisories, incidents and IOCs

item.title: [HIGH] Ransomware: qilin claims EFU Life Assurance
item.description: Ransomware group 'qilin' claimed victim: EFU Life Assurance. Country: PK. Sector: Financial Services.

item.title: [LOW] Fedora: libseccomp-2.6.1-2.fc43 (FEDORA-2026-9afc6b6bb3)
item.description: # `libseccomp` 2.6.1 ## packaging changes - Enable Python bindings on Fedora 44+ ## upstream changes - July 1, 2026 - Update the syscall table for Linux v7.1.0-rc4 - Fix incorrect 64-bit comparison merge that can weaken libseccomp filters. See GitHub Advisory [GHSA-4q85-33p6-j5g6](https://github.com/seccomp/libseccomp/security/advisories/GHSA-4q85-33p6-j5g6) - Fix issue where oversized libsecc

item.title: [MEDIUM] Fedora: btrbk-0.32.7-1.fc44 (FEDORA-2026-131c82812a)
item.description: Update to 0.32.7 (RHBZ#2502632) which includes fix for CVE-2026-62943

item.title: [MEDIUM] Fedora: nodejs22-22.23.1-2.fc45 (FEDORA-2026-3298e71891)
item.description: Automatic update for nodejs22-22.23.1-2.fc45. ##### **Changelog** ``` * Mon Jul 20 2026 tjuhasz - 1:22.23.1-2 - CVE-2026-42338 ip-address HTML escaping fix (rhbz#2487625) * Mon Jul 20 2026 tjuhasz - 1:22.23.1-1 - Update to version 22.23.1 (rhbz#2477273). * Thu Jul 16 2026 Fedora Release Engineering - 1:22.22.2-4 - Rebuilt for h

item.title: [LOW] Fedora: kronosnet-1.35-1.fc45 (FEDORA-2026-3e85d87212)
item.description: Automatic update for kronosnet-1.35-1.fc45. ##### **Changelog** ``` * Mon Jul 20 2026 Fabio M. Di Nitto - 1.35-1 - New upstream release - CVE-2026-15811 (LOW): encryption key exposure in memory after cryptographic configuration changes. Wipe cryptographic keys with explicit_bzero() before freeing to prevent exposure through memory disclosure vulnerabilities. (Resolves rhbz#

item.title: [HIGH] Fedora: nginx-1.30.4-1.fc45 (FEDORA-2026-d1cd8e3d25)
item.description: nginx-mod-fancyindex: Rebuild for 1.30.4 nginx-mod-modsecurity: Rebuild for 1.30.4 nginx-mod-naxsi: Rebuild for 1.30.4 nginx-mod-headers-more: Rebuild for 1.30.4 nginx-mod-brotli: Rebuild for 1.30.4 nginx-mod-js-challenge: Rebuild for 1.30.4 nginx-mod-vts: Rebuild for 1.30.4 nginx: update to 1.30.4 fixes CVE-2026-42533, CVE-2026-60005, CVE-2026-56

item.title: [MEDIUM] Fedora: kernel-7.1.4-202.fc44 (FEDORA-2026-2dd8b600bb)
item.description: The 7.1.4-102/202 stable kernel updates contain a few important fixes across the tree. ---- The 7.1.4-101/201 stable kernel update contains a fix for a rather important security issue with net/can.

item.title: [MEDIUM] Fedora: kernel-7.1.4-102.fc43 (FEDORA-2026-f1fc7772c3)
item.description: The 7.1.4-102/202 stable kernel updates contain a few important fixes across the tree. ---- The 7.1.4-101/201 stable kernel update contains a fix for a rather important security issue with net/can.

item.description: Scan Windows Domain Controllers for CVE-2026-41089 to detect unauthenticated remote code execution vulnerabilities in the Netlogon service.

item.title: [CRITICAL] PoC released: CVE-2012-1823
item.description: This repository documents the process of identifying, analyzing, and gathering Open Source Intelligence (OSINT) on a specific security vulnerability detected during a target network scan.

item.title: [CRITICAL] PoC released: CVE-2026-58138
item.description: CVE-2026-58138 - Conductor (3.21.21..<3.30.2) unauthenticated RCE via INLINE GraalVM evaluator

item.title: [HIGH] PoC released: CVE-2023-21817
item.description: A minimalist Local Privilege Escalation (LPE) vulnerability checker for CVE-2023-21817 (Windows Kerberos LPE) written in C++.

item.title: [MEDIUM] PoC released: CVE-2025-64512
item.description: Exploit for CVE-2025-64512 to get a reverse shell.

item.title: [MEDIUM] VMware: VMSA-2026-0004: VMware Cloud Foundation Operations updates address multiple vulnerabilities (CVE-2026-41722, CVE-2026-41723 and CVE-2026-41724)
[MEDIUM] CVE-2026-15517: A flaw has been found in Jinher OA 1.0. The affected element is an unknown funct...
item.description: A flaw has been found in Jinher OA 1.0. The affected element is an unknown function of the file /C6/JHSoft.Web.PlanSummarize/PlanGiveOut.aspx. This manipulation of the argument httpOID causes sql injection. Remote exploitation of the attack is possible. The exploit has been published and may be used. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

item.title: [HIGH] CVE-2026-15516: A vulnerability was detected in MacCMS Pro up to 2022.1000.3005. Impacted is the...
item.description: A vulnerability was detected in MacCMS Pro up to 2022.1000.3005. Impacted is the function step5 of the file application/install/controller/Index.php of the component Installation Module. The manipulation results in authorization bypass. The attack may be launched remotely. The attack requires a high level of complexity. The exploitability is considered difficult. The exploit is now public and may

item.title: [HIGH] CVE-2026-15515: A security vulnerability has been detected in Tencent PC Manager 18.1.30242.301....
item.description: A security vulnerability has been detected in Tencent PC Manager 18.1.30242.301. This issue affects some unknown processing in the library qmudisk64.sys of the component QMUDisk Driver. The manipulation leads to uncontrolled search path. The attack must be carried out locally. The attack is considered to have high complexity. The exploitability is assessed as difficult. The exploit has been disclo

item.title: [MEDIUM] CVE-2026-15514: A weakness has been identified in Metasoft 美特软件 MetaCRM up to 6.4.0 Beta06. This...
item.description: A weakness has been identified in Metasoft 美特软件 MetaCRM up to 6.4.0 Beta06. This vulnerability affects the function RPCService.query of the file /customizemt/xkq/rpc.jsp of the component PHPRPC Remote Call Interface. Executing a manipulation of the argument phprpc_args can lead to sql injection. The attack can be launched remotely. The exploit has been made available to the public and could be use

item.title: [MEDIUM] CVE-2026-15513: A security flaw has been discovered in Wavlink WL-NU516U1 260515. This affects t...
item.description: A security flaw has been discovered in Wavlink WL-NU516U1 260515. This affects the function wlink_uci_set_value of the file /cgi-bin/adm.cgi. Performing a manipulation of the argument lan_ip results in os command injection. The attack can be initiated remotely. The exploit has been released to the public and may be used for attacks. You should upgrade the affected component. The vendor was contact

item.title: [MEDIUM] CVE-2026-15512: A vulnerability was identified in pig-mesh Pig up to 3.9.2. Affected by this iss...
item.description: A vulnerability was identified in pig-mesh Pig up to 3.9.2. Affected by this issue is some unknown functionality of the file \pig-master\pig-visual\pig-codegen\src\main\java\com\pig4cloud\pig\codegen\service\impl\GeneratorServiceImpl.java of the component pig-codegen. Such manipulation leads to code injection. It is possible to launch the attack remotely. The exploit is publicly available and migh

item.title: [MEDIUM] CVE-2026-15511: A vulnerability was determined in Comfast CF-WR631AX V3 up to 2.7.0.8. Affected ...
item.description: A vulnerability was determined in Comfast CF-WR631AX V3 up to 2.7.0.8. Affected by this vulnerability is the function system_wl_upload_pic_file of the file /usr/bin/webmgnt of the component FastCGI Backend. This manipulation of the argument filename causes os command injection. It is possible to initiate the attack remotely. The exploit has been publicly disclosed and may be utilized. The vendor w

item.title: [MEDIUM] CVE-2026-15510: A vulnerability was found in Leantime up to 3.8.0. Affected is the function Sett...
item.description: A vulnerability was found in Leantime up to 3.8.0. Affected is the function Setting::saveSetting of the component API. The manipulation results in improper authorization. The attack may be performed from remote. The exploit has been made public and could be used. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

item.title: [MEDIUM] CVE-2026-15509: A vulnerability has been found in Leantime up to 3.8.0. This impacts the functio...
item.description: A vulnerability has been found in Leantime up to 3.8.0. This impacts the function editUser/addUser of the component JSON-RPC Endpoint. The manipulation of the argument role leads to improper authorization. The attack is possible to be carried out remotely. The exploit has been disclosed to the public and may be used. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any w

item.title: [MEDIUM] CVE-2026-15508: A flaw has been found in Helicone ai-gateway up to 0.2.0-beta.30. This affects t...
item.description: A flaw has been found in Helicone ai-gateway up to 0.2.0-beta.30. This affects the function build_target_url of the file ai-gateway/src/dispatcher/service.rs of the component AWS Metadata Service. Executing a manipulation of the argument extracted_path_and_query can lead to server-side request forgery. The attack can be executed remotely. The exploit has been published and may be used. The vendor

item.title: [MEDIUM] Spamhaus DROP: 1.10.16.0/20
item.description: Malicious IP range: 1.10.16.0/20 — SBL256894

item.title: [MEDIUM] GHSA: Eclipse Jetty: HTTP Authority/Host mismatch
item.description: #### Summary Jetty currently accepts HTTP/2 and HTTP/3 requests where the regular Host header and the pseudo-header :authority do not match. As a result, the same request can carry two different host identities through Jetty: - logic based on `HttpURI` / `Request.getServerName(request)` uses `:authority` - logic based on raw request headers continues to use `Host` This creates a host/authority con

item.title: [MEDIUM] GHSA: Eclipse Jetty: Cross-Request Leakage for trailers on HTTP/1.1 keep-alive connections
item.description: ### Description > FINDING — MEDIUM (HTTP/1.1 keep-alive connections with trailers) > HttpConnection._trailers Cross-Request Leakage (Never Reset Between Requests) > > Location: > jetty-core/jetty-server/src/main/java/org/eclipse/jetty/server/internal/ > HttpConnection.java:107, 1157-1161, 1170 > > Detail: > _trailers (line 107) is a connection-scoped HttpFields.Mutable field. > parsedTrailer() (li

item.title: [HIGH] GHSA: Eclipse Jetty Digest Authentication: ISO-8859-1 lossy encoding allows authentication bypass via character substitution
item.description: ### Summary The `DigestAuthentication.apply()` method in Jetty's HTTP client uses `getBytes(StandardCharsets.ISO_8859_1)` at three locations (lines 171, 179, 196) to compute Digest auth response hashes. ISO-8859-1 silently replaces any character above U+00FF (Chinese, Japanese, Cyrillic, Arabic, Emoji, etc.) with 0x3F (`?`), causing all such characters to produce identical hash contributions. An a

item.title: [LOW] GHSA: n8n: Custom Header Credential Values Leaked in Plaintext into LLM Node Execution Data
item.description: ## Impact Custom HTTP headers configured in credentials for certain LLM sub-nodes (including OpenAI, Anthropic, and Lemonade) are masked in the n8n UI but are written in plaintext into execution data during workflow runs. Any authenticated user with access to the execution data for an affected workflow can read the header names and values, which typically contain API keys or other secrets. Because

item.title: [MEDIUM] GHSA: Dompdf: Embedded SVG images can leak existence of files and directories within the filesystem
item.description: ### Summary If a malicious actor can supply unrestricted content for rendering by Dompdf they can utilize the SVG rendering functionality to leak filesystem information when rendering PDF files using image references within a data-URI encoded SVG document. ### Details Using an `` element inside a data-URI embedded SVG, an attacker can attempt to embed other files via the `href` or `xlink:href` att

item.title: [CRITICAL] GHSA: Dompdf: Denial of Service (DoS) via Resource Exhaustion using Oversized Image Bitmaps
item.description: ### Summary Dompdf v3.1.5 is vulnerable to a Denial of Service (DoS) attack via resource exhaustion. An attacker can crash the PHP process by providing a specially crafted HTML document containing a single image with massive dimensions (e.g., 30,000x30,000 pixels). While Dompdf implements internal checks to validate image dimensions, these can be bypassed by using a high-entropy image (such as ran

item.title: [CRITICAL] GHSA: Dompdf: Uncontrolled resource consumption based on declared BMP dimensions
item.description: ### Summary dompdf accepts a BMP image and generates a PDF-compatible PNG based only on its *declared* header dimensions and never bounds width × height before the image is converted through GD. A 58-byte BMP whose header declares e.g. `6000×6000` is accepted and later drives `imagecreatetruecolor($width, $height)` (and PHP's native BMP decoder) to allocate the full pixel canvas. A payload can fit

item.title: [MEDIUM] GHSA: LiteLLM: Custom Code Guardrails production endpoints bypass code safety checks
item.description: ### Impact LiteLLM's Custom Code Guardrails production create/update paths did not apply the same sandboxing and validation used by the test endpoint. A privileged user with access to create or update guardrails could submit custom Python code that executed in the LiteLLM proxy environment. In deployments without a configured master key, callers could be treated as proxy administrators, making thi

item.title: [CRITICAL] GHSA: LiteLLM: MCP Authentication Bypass via OAuth2 Passthrough Fallback
item.description: ### Impact LiteLLM's MCP Streamable HTTP endpoint could allow an unauthenticated attacker to establish an authenticated MCP session using an arbitrary Bearer token. The MCP auth handler supported OAuth2 passthrough for upstream MCP servers, but the fallback path could replace failed LiteLLM key validation with an empty `UserAPIKeyAuth()` object. This allowed requests with a fabricated `Authorizati

item.title: [MEDIUM] GHSA: LiteLLM: Local file read via request-supplied OIDC file references
item.description: ### Impact LiteLLM's `/health/test_connection` endpoint resolved request-supplied environment and OIDC file references in `litellm_params`. A proxy administrator, or another privileged caller with permission to test model connections, could cause LiteLLM to read files from the local filesystem via an `oidc/file/` reference. Because exploitation requires privileged proxy access, this is treated as

item.title: [CRITICAL] GitHub issues $100,000 bounty for critical RCE vulnerability
item.description: submitted by   /u/ryanmerket [link]   [comments]

item.title: [MEDIUM] Upbound says hack caused $13 million in fraudulent Acima leases
item.description: The Upbound Group fintech company disclosed that threat actors who stole data from its systems leveraged it to create $13 million in Acima leases. [...]

item.title: [MEDIUM] Attackers Are Learning to Live Off the AI Toolchain
item.description: Sandworm_Mode is an early example of malware that exploits trusted AI tools and workflows to make malicious activity virtually indistinguishable from normal activity.
